metadata.greeting: This weekend in London, there are so many exciting things to do! From family mornings at the Affordable Art Fair in Battersea Park to puppet shows based on Eric Carle's books in Blackheath. Get ready for a weekend filled with fun and adventure!

activities.title: Inside Aardman: Wallace & Gromit and Friends
activities.description: Exhibition showcasing the work of Aardman Animations, from idea development and storyboarding to model making and filming.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits will engage a 3-year-old, and the characters are familiar and fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green
transport.tip: The museum is a short walk from the station.

activities.title: Library Club
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed environment with books suitable for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Buses and Overground to Hackney Downs or Hackney Central
transport.tip: Check term times before travelling.

activities.title: Victoria Park
activities.description: Large park with ponds and children's play areas, including giant slides and a paddling pool.
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of space to run around, plus playground equipment.

transport.fromHackney: Buses or walk to Victoria Park
transport.tip: Pack a picnic to enjoy in the park.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore the history of homes and domestic life.
activities.whyItWorks: Interesting displays and exhibits that can spark curiosity.

transport.fromHackney: Buses to Hoxton
transport.tip: Check for family trails.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A city farm with a community feel, featuring various animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Kids love seeing animals up close.

transport.fromHackney: Buses to Brick Lane
transport.tip: Check for special Easter activities.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground. The museum hosts Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Immersive play and activities designed for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Stratford
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm with animals and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Local and easy to visit for a quick outing with animals.

transport.fromHackney: Walk or bus
transport.tip: Check for workshops.

activities.title: Half Moon Theatre: Tiny Planet
activities.description: A theatre production for children.
activities.whyItWorks: Live performance can be engaging for older toddlers. Note age range is 5+, may not be suitable.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Limehouse
transport.tip: Check showtimes and book in advance.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore the history of planet Earth with dinosaur fossils and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs, animals, and interactive exhibits captivate young children.

transport.fromHackney: Piccadilly Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with hands-on exhibits and space exploration galleries.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits are perfect for curious toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Piccadilly Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: The Garden in the basement is great for under 5s.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: Indoor garden with panoramic views of the city.
activities.whyItWorks: Great views and greenery create a sensory experience.

transport.fromHackney: Various options, check TfL for best route. Usually involves a train and tube
transport.tip: Book tickets well in advance as they are popular.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore world art and artifacts.
activities.whyItWorks: Fascinating objects to spark curiosity.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Holborn or Tottenham Court Road
transport.tip: Pick a specific gallery to focus on to avoid overwhelm.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Small zoo in Hampstead Heath with rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, free entry, and playground in the same area.

transport.fromHackney: Northern Line to Golders Green
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated entirely to children, with an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool, and a small city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space to play and explore in central London.

transport.fromHackney: Various options, check TfL for best route.
transport.tip: Paddling pool is open in the summer months.

activities.title: Changing of the Guard
activities.description: Traditional ceremony with marching and music.
activities.whyItWorks: The spectacle is engaging for children.

transport.fromHackney: Various options, check TfL for best route.
transport.tip: Check the schedule in advance as it doesn't happen every day.

activities.title: Southbank Centre
activities.description: Riverside hub of arts, fun and food with free installations and open spaces.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots to see and do, with space to roam.

transport.fromHackney: Various options, check TfL for best route.
transport.tip: Check the calendar for free family events.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Easter fun and chocolate.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Hampton Court
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the park.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals and Easter eggs.

transport.fromHackney: Various options, check TfL for best route.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Octonauts Above and Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.description: Join the Octonauts on an exciting outdoor mission at Kew Gardens. An interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive trail with a nature theme.

transport.fromHackney: Various options, check TfL for best route.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: British Library's Fairy Tales exhibition
activities.description: Explore enchanted lands, magical creatures, iconic characters and timeless tales, brought to life through books, theatre costumes, puppets, pop-ups, artwork and illustrations.
activities.whyItWorks: Costumes, puppets, and pop-ups will engage toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Various options, check TfL for best route.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Your Toys
activities.description: Children are invited to bring along a toy to lend for the performance and watch it come to life on stage, plus there's 10 minutes of playtime at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive theatre experience.

transport.fromHackney: Various options, check TfL for best route.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Battersea Power Station Mr Men Little Miss Takeover
activities.description: Step into the vibrant world of Mr Men/Little Miss through a series of interactive activities, including a treasure hunt where families can use a special map to discover familiar characters.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and engaging for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Various options, check TfL for best route.
transport.tip: Check times and book in advance

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.description: Heartwarming and hilarious adventure.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppet show aimed at young children.

transport.fromHackney: Bus and Tube
transport.tip: Book in advance.

activities.title: The Big Egg Hunt
activities.description: This Easter, Elephant Family and Clarence Court bring a city-wide spectacle to London! Over 100 stunning 2ft egg sculptures, designed by top artists and brands hidden across iconic locations. Use the free app to track them, unlock prizes, and support conservation efforts in this unmissable public art trail.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun, free and engaging

transport.fromHackney: Various
transport.tip: Download the app to participate
